confirmedFindings.title: The Naoyuki rival→respect→brotherhood arc is starved in v1: he vanishes for all of T1 (~8h), the 'respect' stage is never beated, and v1 delivers only a single G5 ally-flip before the brotherhood payoff is cut to T4
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md §1.8 (line 497), §5 T0.5 (lines 3212-3213), §3.6.1 G4→G5 (line 2059), §5 T4.5 (line 3805); NO Naoyuki mention anywhere in T1 (§3.4 V0-V7 lines 1906-1947, §5 T1 lines 3258-3433)
confirmedFindings.evidence: The arc is seeded as three stages — §1.8 (497) 'Arc: rivalry → grudging respect → brotherhood'; §5 T0.5 (3213) 'grudging vouch in T0 that plants the rivalry → respect → brotherhood arc.' A grep for 'Naoyuki' returns hits in T0 (R4 vouch) and T2 (G5) and T3/T4 — but ZERO hits anywhere in T1's 8 hours. The sole v1 development is the abrupt G5 flip: §3.6.1 (2059) 'heir **Naoyuki** now turns from internal rival into **ally against Tomita**'. The brotherhood payoff is T4 (roadmap, not built): §5 T4.5 (3805) 'the rivalry→brotherhood pays off as he reports back from Edo.'
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: The prompt's foreshadow→payoff test: the T0-T2 portion of a deferred arc must be satisfying on its own because T3 is a stub. Here it is not. A 3-stage character arc is heavily seeded in T0, then the character disappears for the entire middle tier, then 'respect' is skipped and the relationship jumps straight to 'ally against Tomita' at ~22h with no connective tissue earning the change — and the actual payoff (brotherhood) is cut. The G5 flip will read as unearned (a rival who suddenly cooperates) because nothing in T1 or early T2 warms the rivalry to respect first.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Add at least one interstitial Naoyuki beat in v1 (e.g. a T1 or early-T2 moment where the rivalry visibly thaws toward respect) so the G5 ally-flip is earned, and confirm whether 'rival → ally' is an acceptable v1 landing for this arc given the brotherhood is cut to T4.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Factual claims verified against docs/prd.md. The Naoyuki arc is heavily seeded and LOCKED as three stages — §1.8 line 497 ("Arc: rivalry → grudging respect → brotherhood") and §5 T0.5 line 3213 ("grudging vouch in T0 that plants the rivalry → respect → brotherhood arc"). A scan of the entire T1 tier confirms ZERO Naoyuki presence: the T1 unlock ladder (§3.4, lines 1875-1950) and the full T1 narrative (§5 T1, lines 3258-3433) contain no mention of him — the only "rival" references there are the distant Tomita/Akagi houses, not Naoyuki. His sole v1 development is the abrupt G5 flip (§3.6.1 line 2059: "heir Naoyuki now turns from internal rival into ally against Tomita"; echoed §5 T2 line 3538). The brotherhood payoff is explicitly cut to the T4 roadmap (line 3815). So the middle of a heavily-seeded arc is starved: the character vanishes for all of T1 (~8h), the "respect" stage gets no dedicated on-screen beat, and the relationship jumps rivalry→ally at ~22h with no dramatized warming. This is a legitimate narrative-pacing/emotion gap, not reconciled elsewhere in the doc. It is NOT trivial-apply: whether to add an interstitial beat vs. accept "rival → ally → brotherhood-deferred" as the v1 landing is a story/scope tradeoff requiring the human's call. One caveat lowering confidence: the G5 ally-flip can be read as the "grudging respect" stage landing (a rival who grudgingly becomes an ally IS grudging respect made concrete), and the T2 climb itself — where the MC visibly outpaces Naoyuki, his converted-not-innate growth mirroring the thesis (line 3538) — arguably supplies thematic connective tissue. That weakens the "respect never beated / unearned" framing but does not erase the core observation that he is wholly absent for the entire middle tier.
confirmedFindings.question: The Naoyuki rivalry→respect→brotherhood arc is LOCKED and heavily seeded in T0 (§1.8 line 497; §5 T0.5 line 3213), but he then appears ZERO times in all of T1 (§3.4 ladder + §5 T1 narrative, lines 1875-3433) and gets only one v1 beat — the G5 ally-flip "now turns from internal rival into ally against Tomita" (§3.6.1 line 2059) — with brotherhood cut to the T4 roadmap (line 3815). Do you want an interstitial Naoyuki beat added in v1 (e.g. a T1 or early-T2 moment where the rivalry visibly thaws toward grudging respect) so the G5 ally-flip reads as earned rather than abrupt? Options: (A) Add one cheap dialogue/quest beat in T1 or early T2 to dramatize the 'respect' stage and bridge the ~8h gap; (B) Accept 'seed in T0 → ally-flip at T2-G5 → brotherhood deferred to T4' as the intended v1 landing and treat the G5 flip itself as the respect payoff, relying on the MC's T2 climb (which visibly outpaces Naoyuki) as the implicit warming. Tradeoff: (A) costs authored content but protects the emotional payoff of a locked character arc; (B) keeps v1 scope tight but risks the flip reading as unearned after the character disappears for an entire tier.

confirmedFindings.title: The §6.6 verifier checks ids + canon flags but ZERO numeric/structural balance invariants — so the only release gate cannot catch the round-1 impossible-gate OR the deed-count inconsistency
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md §6.6 lines 4119-4130 (machine-check list) & §2.20(c) line 1640 (VerifyReport)
confirmedFindings.evidence: §6.6's enumerated machine checks are entirely id/flag/lint shaped: belief-creature-not-in-spawn (4121), trade ≤⅓ (4122), ≤1 ambiguity (4123), 'no Influence path is a passive per-tick trickle or flat per-action increment' (4124), 'no content grants a combat stat/training-rate bonus from labour conditioning' (4125), macron lint (4126-4130). There is NO check that gate thresholds are ordered/reachable, and NO check that the accrual arithmetic ties out. Round-1 numbers-balance-1 (R6/E2 requires Estate ≥ 1K, exceeding the very next T0→T1 gate of 0.8K — impossible on an up-only curve) is a pure threshold-monotonicity bug a 3-line static check over ranks.ts+influence.ts would have flagged automatically. Round-1 intent-fidelity-1 (T0 Arms deed counts cannot produce the stated Arms ip) is a deed-inventory-sum bug a registry-arithmetic check would have flagged. Neither is in the verifier; both were found only by a human READING. §2.20(c) names 'unreachableReveals[]' in the VerifyReport but §6.6 never specifies its algorithm and numeric-threshold reachability is not covered — the field over-promises.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: verify is the ONLY release gate (no hosted CI). The two most design-breaking bugs round-1 found are both statically machine-checkable from the registries, yet both would slip through verify today and only surface (if at all) as an M3+ auto-player stall in a 28.5 h run no human hand-plays. These checks are cheap and strengthen the gate without trade-off.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Add two bullets to §6.6's machine-check list: (1) gate monotonicity & ceiling — within a tier every rung's per-pillar gating threshold is non-decreasing and never exceeds that pillar's tier-up gate; (2) accrual arithmetic ties out — each pillar's deed inventory sums to the locked ~70% of its §4.1 gate, every deed base ≤ its 0.04·band-top cap, and each tier's per-rung gating costs sum to the locked hour budget.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason:
Factually accurate: §6.6's machine-check list (prd.md lines 4121-4130) contains only id/flag/lint checks (belief-creature exclusion, trade ≤⅓, ≤1 ambiguity, no-passive-Influence, no labour→combat, macron lint). No static check asserts gate-threshold monotonicity/reachability or that deed-accrual arithmetic ties out; the §4.2.1 70%-share itemizations (lines 2404-2431) are hand-✔'d in prose, not registry-asserted; §2.20(c) line 1640's `unreachableReveals[]` has no specified algorithm and omits numeric reachability. Genuinely NEW vs round-1 (round-1 found the specific bugs by human reading; this targets the verifier's failure to catch the class). Partially mitigated, hence not high: a RUNTIME headless pacing gate (lines 4391-4394, 4694) already enforces per-tier hour budgets + ≥30-min floor (overlapping the accrual point but not monotonicity/deed-sums), and the thresholds are explicitly 'proposed v1 balance — for human review.' needs-human, not trivial-apply: §4.2.1 calls the exact 70/30 split a 'tunable proposed realisation' with ~20-35 deed / ~20-35% trade ranges, so whether to encode it as a hard-equality assert vs a tolerance, and whether ranks.ts carries per-rung pillar thresholds to make monotonicity assertable, are design/scope judgements on the sole release gate — not a clerical edit.</verdict_reason>
<parameter name="refined_question">Should §6.6's content-verifier machine-check list gain two static balance invariants asserted directly from the ranks.ts/influence.ts registries: (1) gate monotonicity & ceiling — within each tier every rung's per-pillar gating threshold is non-decreasing and never exceeds that pillar's §4.1 tier-up gate (catches impossible-gate bugs); and (2) accrual arithmetic ties out — each pillar's deed inventory sums to its locked ~70% gate share, every deed base ≤ its 0.04·band-top cap, and per-rung gating costs sum to the locked hour budget (catches deed-count inconsistencies)? Trade-offs: (a) coverage — these are cheap static checks that would catch two design-breaking bug classes round-1 caught only by human reading, before they surface as an M3+ auto-player stall; but (b) the existing runtime M6/M7 pacing gate already enforces hour budgets at playtest time, so check (2)'s hour-budget clause partly duplicates it; and (c) §4.2.1 explicitly labels the 70/30 split a 'tunable proposed realisation' with ~20-35-deed and ~20-35% ranges — so a hard-equality '=70%' assert risks brittleness and the human must decide an exact tolerance, plus confirm ranks.ts actually stores per-rung pillar thresholds for the monotonicity check to be machine-evaluable.</refined_question>
</invoke>
confirmedFindings.old_string: - no content grants a combat stat/training-rate bonus from labour conditioning (no labour→combat cross-feed);

confirmedFindings.title: generate-don't-duplicate structurally cannot cover the PRD's own §4 — gen-docs writes only docs/balance/+docs/content/, never docs/prd.md, so every hand-typed derived number in §4 drifts undetected
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md §6.6 lines 4132-4137 (gen-docs targets) vs §4.9 JUDGE_K table (3054-3056), §4.2.1 itemizations (2408-2429), §4.5.1 XP figures (2692-2693)
confirmedFindings.evidence: §6.6: gen-docs 'writes balance/content tables into docs/ (e.g. docs/balance/curves.md, docs/content/bestiary.md, docs/content/areas.md, docs/content/ranks.md, docs/content/influence.md). Run with --check ... to fail the build if the committed docs drift from the data.' The generated targets are NEW files under docs/balance/ and docs/content/ — gen-docs never touches docs/prd.md. But §4 of the PRD is itself dense with HAND-TYPED derived values: the §4.9 'derived JUDGE_K[pillar][tier] = SEASONAL_SHARE · gate table (T0 Arms 150 / Estate 240 · T1 ... Name 4,500 · T2 ... Name 42,000)'; the §4.2.1 deed itemizations ('30 + 224 + 234 + 72 = 560 ✔'); the §4.5.1 curve examples ('L10 cumulatively ~1.2K, L25 ~17K, L50 ~1.1M'); the §4.8 wall-clock sums. Round-1 numbers-balance-2 (JUDGE_K listed as 3,000/24,000 vs the 4,500/42,000 derivation) and numbers-balance-3 (stale XP figures) are EXACTLY this class — and were fixed by hand, not by a generator. gen:docs --check enforces balance.ts ⇄ docs/balance/curves.md, but never balance.ts ⇄ PRD §4.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: The single most drift-prone document — the balance spec — is the one NOT generated. Drift between §4 prose and balance.ts will recur every retune and is invisible to the gate, re-creating exactly the round-1 JUDGE_K/XP drift. The convention the project leans on is silently not applied to its own authoritative spec.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Decide the policy: either (a) mark all §4 numeric tables as illustrative-only with a banner pointing to the generated docs/balance/ as authoritative (and stop hand-maintaining numbers in §4), or (b) extend gen-docs to emit the §4 derived tables (JUDGE_K, deed itemizations, XP example points, pacing sums) into a generated include that --check guards.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Cited text is accurate: §6.6 (4132-4137) scopes gen-docs to docs/balance/ + docs/content/ only, and §4.9 (3054-3056) hand-types the derived JUDGE_K table next to its own formula. The finding's strong framing ("the convention structurally cannot cover its own §4") is overstated and partly misframes the convention: the PRD explicitly makes balance.ts SUBORDINATE to §4 ("§4 sets the values" L4105; balance.ts holds "§4 constants by reference ... no restated/invented numbers" L4497/4656/4693), and the docs-taxonomy treats the PRD as the human-authored design spec, not a generated artifact. So §4 is the upstream source, balance.ts references it, docs/balance/ is generated from balance.ts and gated — there is no parallel hand-maintained shipping copy that drifts undetected; that path is guarded by the no-invented-numbers rule. The real residual kernel: §4.9 longhand-types DERIVED values (JUDGE_K = SEASONAL_SHARE·gate) beside their formula, and round-1 proved those can disagree with the derivation (numbers-balance-2/-3). That intra-§4 derivation-vs-typed drift is genuinely ungated and recurs per retune, so a policy decision is warranted — but it is medium, not high, and is a needs-human policy choice, not the structural defect claimed. Marginally NEW: it is a process/meta finding distinct from the specific number-fixes of round-1, though it openly builds on them.
confirmedFindings.question: §4 of the PRD is the human-authored design spec; the project already requires balance.ts to REFERENCE §4 ("no invented/restated numbers") and gates only registries↔docs/balance/ via gen:docs --check. The residual risk is that §4 itself contains hand-typed DERIVED values (notably the §4.9 JUDGE_K[pillar][tier] table = SEASONAL_SHARE·gate, the §4.2.1 deed-sum itemizations, §4.5.1 XP example points, §4.8 wall-clock sums) typed longhand next to their formulas — exactly the spot where round-1 found JUDGE_K (3,000/24,000 vs the 4,500/42,000 derivation) and stale XP drift, with no automated gate. Which policy do you want: (a) mark all §4 *derived/illustrative* numeric tables with a banner declaring them illustrative-of-the-formula and pointing to generated docs/balance/ as canonical, and stop hand-maintaining the longhand results; or (b) extend gen-docs to emit the §4 derived tables (JUDGE_K, deed-sum checks, XP example points, pacing sums) into a generated include that gen:docs --check guards against balance.ts; or (c) accept §4 as a hand-maintained design spec and rely on round-by-round review (status quo)?

confirmedFindings.title: Touring Inspector 'Toyama Saemon-no-jō' is a real historical magistrate — a real-name leak beyond round-1's Nihonbashi
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md §1.8 line 551; §5 T4.2 lines 3782-3783; §5 T4.3 line 3795
confirmedFindings.evidence: §1.8 L551: "**Junkenshi Toyama Saemon-no-jō (the Touring Inspector)** | A senior shogunal inspector." Repeated at L3782-3783 ("**Junkenshi Toyama / Saemon-no-jō** will survey the domain") and L3795 ("**The Touring Inspector (Toyama Saemon-no-jō)**"). 'Tōyama Saemon-no-jō' is the documented formal name of Tōyama Kagemoto (遠山左衛門尉景元, 1793–1855), the famous Edo machi-bugyō universally known in jidaigeki as 'Tōyama no Kin-san.' Pairing his exact name with a shogunal-inspector role makes the real-person reference unmistakable. This violates A6/canon §A ("kept FICTIONAL. No real place/daimyo names", prd_human_feedback A6; §1.3 L133 "kept fictional — no real place or daimyō names"; §1.13 L744 "no real place/daimyō/house names"). It is also a *period* slip: Kagemoto is early-19th-c., outside the 'mid-Edo, ~18th c.' frame.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: A6 is a hard, human-locked constraint; a famous real magistrate's name at the game's emotional capstone is the most conspicuous possible violation and will read as a recognisable reference to any Japanese player.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Rename the inspector to a wholly invented surname + a generic court title (the office 'junkenshi' and a title like 'Saemon-no-jō' are fine generically; the surname 'Toyama' is the leak). Confirm whether a Kin-san homage was intended (cf. the 'Kurosawa' house name) — if so, deliberately blur it; if not, swap outright.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Confirmed verbatim: docs/prd.md L551 "Junkenshi Toyama Saemon-no-jō (the Touring Inspector)", L3782-3783 "Junkenshi Toyama / Saemon-no-jō", L3795 "The Touring Inspector (Toyama Saemon-no-jō)". The name+title "Tōyama Saemon-no-jō" is the documented formal name of Tōyama Kagemoto, the famous Edo machi-bugyō universally known in jidaigeki as "Tōyama no Kin-san"; the PRD even casts him in the matching wise-impartial-judge role, so the reference is recognizable. This conflicts with the human-locked "Fictionalise real names" rule (prd_human_feedback A6; prd.md L744 heading; L133 "kept fictional"). It is genuinely NEW (round-1 caught Nihonbashi, a place; this is a person-name leak). Marked needs-human (not refuted) because: (a) the literal rule wording enumerates "place/daimyō/house names" and a hatamoto magistrate is none of those, so whether the rule's spirit captures it is a judgement call; (b) the protagonist house "Kurosawa" plausibly is itself an intentional homage, so a Kin-san nod may be deliberate — which is exactly the design decision a human must make. The minor period slip (Kagemoto 1793-1855 vs ~18th-c. frame) is secondary. Severity high because it sits on the apex emotional-capstone character against a hard human-locked constraint; confidence medium because intent (homage vs leak) is genuinely ambiguous.
confirmedFindings.question: The T4 touring inspector is named "Junkenshi Toyama Saemon-no-jō" (prd.md L551, L3782-3783, L3795). "Tōyama Saemon-no-jō" is the real Edo magistrate Tōyama Kagemoto ("Tōyama no Kin-san"), and his real-life role (wise impartial judge) matches the character's. The human-locked rule "Fictionalise real names" (A6) forbids real names. Was this an intended Kin-san homage (cf. the possibly-deliberate "Kurosawa" house name)? Options: (1) swap the surname "Toyama" for a wholly invented one, keeping the generic office "junkenshi" and the generic court title "Saemon-no-jō" (both fine in the abstract); (2) keep it as a deliberate, blurred homage and explicitly note the exception in canon. The office and title are not the leak — only the surname "Toyama" paired with that title is. Which fictional surname should replace it for the apex judge?

confirmedFindings.title: Recurring T4 face 'Rusui Konoe' uses a real premier court-noble (sekke) house name and creates a status anachronism
confirmedFindings.evidence: §1.7.1 L470: "the house **staffs & runs the domain's establishment** (rusui **Konoe**)"; §5 T4.4 L3802: "**Rusui Konoe Settsu-no-suke** (the MC's sole pen-pal proxy in the capital)". 'Konoe' (近衛) is one of the five sekke regent houses of the court nobility (and the surname of 20th-c. PM Konoe Fumimaro) — among the most recognisable real Japanese house names. It is used here, 8+ times, for a provincial domain's *rusui* (residence-keeper), a samurai administrative post a sekke noble would never hold. So the name both (a) violates the FICTIONAL rule (§1.13 L744 'no real place/daimyō/house names') and (b) is a status/mibun anachronism (court-noble house name on a minor samurai functionary).
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: Same hard FICTIONAL constraint as finding 1; because Konoe is the *only* recurring T4 capital face, the leak saturates the entire endgame tier.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Rename to a plain, invented samurai surname (e.g. a neutral two-kanji name with no go-sekke/kuge resonance). Keep 'Settsu-no-suke' (a generic zuryō-style title) if desired.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason:
Confirmed against docs/prd.md. "Konoe" appears 8+ times (L470, 640, 668, 2174, 3755, 3771, 3774, 3780, 3788, 3802) as the surname of the domain's rusui-yaku and is the sole recurring T4 capital proxy ("Rusui Konoe Settsu-no-suke", L3802). Konoe (近衛) is the senior of the five go-sekke regent court-noble houses (and PM Konoe Fumimaro's name) — among the most recognisable real Japanese house names. The PRD sets an explicit hard rule at L744-745 ("no real place/daimyō/house names"; invented Sawatari-juku, fictionalised great-house analogues for banzuke), and the milestone constraint at L4415 reiterates "force-fictionalised real names". Using Konoe (a) violates that FICTIONAL rule and (b) is a status/mibun mismatch (a kuge court-noble house name on a buke samurai functionary post a sekke noble would never hold). Distinct from finding 1 (different name). Renaming the sole recurring endgame capital face is a story/naming judgement, so needs-human. Severity high: the leak saturates the entire endgame tier and breaks the project's #1 authenticity bar via its own stated constraint.</verdict_reason>
<parameter name="refined_question">The recurring T4 capital proxy is named "Rusui Konoe Settsu-no-suke" (Konoe used 8+ times, e.g. prd.md L470, L3802). "Konoe" (近衛) is a real go-sekke regent court-noble house — violating the PRD's own hard rule (L744 "no real place/daimyō/house names") and creating a kuge-name-on-a-buke-functionary status mismatch. What invented two-kanji samurai surname (no go-sekke/kuge resonance) should replace "Konoe"? Keep the generic zuryō-style title "Settsu-no-suke" if desired. This is the sole recurring T4 capital face, so the chosen name will appear throughout the endgame.</refined_question>

confirmedFindings.question: 'Konoe' is a real premier court-noble (sekke) house name, used here for a domain's rusui (a status mismatch). What fictional surname should replace it for the sole recurring T4 capital proxy?

confirmedFindings.title: The 'fictionalise real names' canon rule is the one canon invariant the §6.6 verifier does NOT machine-check — the structural reason real names keep leaking
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md §6.6 lines 4119-4130 (invariant list) vs §1.13 line 744 + docs/history/decisions.md line 112
confirmedFindings.evidence: §6.6 asserts the canon invariants 'as machine checks so they cannot silently rot' and enumerates them (L4121-4130): no belief-creature in spawn tables; trade ≤⅓; ≤1 ambiguity token; no passive Influence trickle; no labour→combat cross-feed; and a *macron* lint. The 'fictionalise real names' rule — explicitly canon at §1.13 L744 ('no real place/daimyō/house names') and decisions.md L112 ('fictionalise real names') — is absent from this list. The only name-related check is the macron-lint, whose allow-list ('shogun, ronin, yokai, samurai, Osaka, daimyō') is a *romanization* allow-list, NOT a fictional-name allow/deny list. With no machine check, real-name leaks (round-1: Nihonbashi at L470/L3772; round-2: Toyama, Konoe; borderline: 'Echizen-ya' one province off the real Echigo-ya/Mitsui) cannot be caught automatically.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: A hard human-locked constraint (A6) is left to manual vigilance while five softer canon rules are machine-enforced; this gap is precisely why a name slipped past round 1 and two more slipped past into round 2.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Add a 7th canon-invariant lint: a denylist of real daimyō/place/temple/historical-person names (+ a small allow-list of intentionally-permitted real terms: Edo, Osaka, Bon, jizō, sankin-kōtai, kaidō, etc.) run over all display/name strings. This is the systemic fix that prevents future leaks.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason:
Factually accurate and genuinely new (meta-audit on verifier coverage, not a single name-fix). Verified at docs/prd.md §6.6 L4119-4130: the machine-checked canon invariants are belief-creature spawn ban, trade ≤⅓, ≤1 ambiguity token, no passive Influence trickle, no labour→combat cross-feed, and a macron lint — and the macron lint's allow-list (shogun/ronin/yokai/samurai/Osaka/daimyō) is a romanization exemption, NOT a real-name denylist. The hard human-locked 'Fictionalise real names' canon (§1.13 L744; re-locked §7.2 L4415) is indeed absent from the machine-check list. Real-world names do leak into T4 roadmap content (Toyama Saemon-no-jō ~ the historical magistrate Tōyama Kagemoto L551/L3782; Konoe, a real regent house; Nihonbashi, a real Edo district L470/L3772; Echizen-ya/Echizen province L552), corroborating that a manual-only rule under-enforces. Severity downgraded from high to medium: §6.6 introduces an explicit *enumerated* list (colon + bullets), so the PRD makes no false claim that ALL canon rules are checked — this is a coverage-gap/hardening suggestion, not an internal contradiction — and the cited leaks sit in the lowest-fidelity T4 roadmap stubs, some plausibly intentional. fix_class is needs-human: a real-name lint requires a curated denylist plus an allow-list of deliberately-permitted real terms (Edo, Osaka, Bon, jizō, sankin-kōtai, kaidō, the inspector/factor surnames if kept), with real trade-offs (false-positive risk, ownership, v1 scope).</verdict_reason>
<parameter name="refined_question">Should the §6.6 content verifier add a 7th canon-invariant machine check — a denylist of real daimyō/house/place/temple/historical-person names run over all display/name strings — to enforce the hard-locked 'Fictionalise real names' canon (§1.13) that is currently the only locked invariant left to manual vigilance? Trade-offs/decisions: (a) it requires a curated allow-list of intentionally-permitted real terms (Edo, Osaka, Bon, jizō, sankin-kōtai, kaidō, kappa, etc.) to avoid false positives, and someone must own/maintain both lists; (b) it would flag current T4-roadmap names (Toyama Saemon-no-jō, Konoe, Nihonbashi, Echizen-ya) — are those to be fictionalised or grandfathered as deliberate? (c) is this a v1 verifier requirement or a v-later hardening, given the leaks are confined to far-future roadmap stubs?
confirmedFindings.question: Should the content verifier enforce 'fictionalise real names' as a machine-checked canon invariant (a real-name denylist + an allow-list of permitted real terms like Edo/Osaka/Bon/jizō), and who curates the lists?

confirmedFindings.title: 'Osaka fudasashi' is a terminology slip — fudasashi were Edo-specific; Osaka's rice-finance brokers were kuramoto/kakeya
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md §1.7.1 line 466 (and §3.7 line 2175)
confirmedFindings.evidence: §1.7.1 L466: "village broker → regional warehouse → *Marutaya* debt-restructuring → *goyōkin* → Osaka/Edo *fudasashi*." §3.7 L2175 repeats "The **Osaka / Edo *fudasashi***". *Fudasashi* (札差) were specifically the Edo (Asakusa) stipend-rice brokers for hatamoto/gokenin; Osaka's rice-market intermediaries for daimyō kurayashiki were *kuramoto* (warehouse managers) and *kakeya* (financial agents). 'Osaka fudasashi' conflates the two.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: Minor but concrete authenticity inaccuracy in the finance-network worldbuilding; the second occurrence at L2175 ('Osaka / Edo fudasashi', with spaces) needs the same correction.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Replace 'Osaka/Edo fudasashi' with 'Osaka kuramoto/kakeya → Edo fudasashi' at both L466 and L2175 (or use a neutral 'the great rice-finance brokers of Osaka and Edo').
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Confirmed at docs/prd.md L466 ("...goyokin -> Osaka/Edo *fudasashi*") and L2175 ("The **Osaka / Edo *fudasashi***"). The historical claim holds: fudasashi (札差) were specifically the Edo/Asakusa stipend-rice brokers for hatamoto/gokenin, while Osaka's daimyo-warehouse rice-finance agents were kuramoto/kakeya, so "Osaka fudasashi" conflates the two. This is reinforced internally: L552 correctly ties fudasashi to Edo ("the Edo factor... *fudasashi*-adjacent"), making "Osaka/Edo fudasashi" an internal inconsistency too. It is genuinely new (a discrete terminology slip). Marked needs-human (not trivial-apply) because: (1) the resolution involves an authenticity/taste choice the suggestion itself frames as alternatives — insert the precise "Osaka kuramoto/kakeya -> Edo fudasashi" chain vs. a neutral "great rice-finance brokers of Osaka and Edo"; and (2) there are two occurrences with different formatting/spacing, so it is not a single byte-exact unique replacement.
confirmedFindings.question: The finance-network worldbuilding labels the brokers "Osaka/Edo fudasashi" (prd.md L466 and L2175), but fudasashi were Edo-specific stipend-rice brokers; Osaka's daimyo-warehouse finance agents were kuramoto/kakeya (and L552 already correctly localizes fudasashi to Edo). How do you want to reconcile both occurrences: (A) precise chain "Osaka *kuramoto/kakeya* -> Edo *fudasashi*", or (B) neutral phrasing "the great rice-finance brokers of Osaka and Edo"? Trade-off: (A) adds authentic specificity but introduces two more period terms the player/reader must absorb; (B) is simpler and avoids the inaccuracy without expanding the glossary.

confirmedFindings.title: Climax fix is INCOMPLETE: the round-1 'climax→spine-guaranteed' fix put the name-reclamation onto the SPINE event-log line, contradicting the locked §1.4/D-006 canon that the name is a side-thread beat (and the same cell's own Origin gating)
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md §3.6 G6 cell line 2027 + §3.6.2 O5 line 2097 vs §1.4 lines 148-149 / D-006 line 771
confirmedFindings.evidence: The round-1 fix correctly moved the Otsuru/Tama truth to the spine, but its G6 SPINE event-log copy (line 2027) — the rung's own diegetic line shown to EVERY player — reads: "...And down-valley: she is real, and grown, and not you. 'Tama ran. Tama lived.' You have a name again: Tahei. You set it down quietly, and pick the house's work back up." That asserts the name-reclamation for all players. Yet the SAME cell's structured field gates it: "[THREAD: Origin] ... Tahei claims his true name (a late, de-emphasised side beat)" on the OPTIONAL Origin track. §3.6.2 O5 (line 2097) confirms the gating — "(Ignoring the Origin track still gives the G6 truth-reveal — you only miss the reunions + buff.)" — i.e. a player who skips Origin is told they do NOT get the name. And §1.4 (lines 148-149) is LOCKED canon: "his true name — Tahei ... returns to him as a late, de-emphasised side beat in the origin thread (identity is explicitly a side thread, not the spine's climax)." D-006 (line 771): "reveal softened to a de-emphasised late side beat." So the spine line directly contradicts both the canon (name = side thread, NOT spine) and the cell's own Origin-gating. Does a player who ignores Origin reclaim 'Tahei' or not? The spine line says yes; canon + O5 say no.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: This is the thematic core of a game literally titled Kami-kakushi (a nameless man reclaiming his identity). The round-1 HIGH finding was marked resolved, but the resolution only handled the Otsuru half — the name half is now self-contradictory at the exact emotional climax. An implementer authoring T2's G6 cannot tell whether to show 'You have a name again: Tahei' to a player who skipped Origin, and either choice breaks a different part of the spec.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Decide whether the name-reclamation is spine-guaranteed or Origin-gated, then make all four sites agree. Canon-default (cheapest, honours §1.4/D-006): drop the name clause from the G6 SPINE event-log line so the spine delivers only the Otsuru truth, leaving 'Tahei sets his true name down' on Origin O5 (where the identical sentence already lives at line 2097). Alternative (if the human wants the name guaranteed to all): move it to spine and update §1.4, D-006, O5's parenthetical, and §3.6.2 line 2088 to match.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Confirmed real and new. The G6 SPINE diegetic event-log line (docs/prd.md:2027) reads "...You have a name again: Tahei. You set it down quietly, and pick the house's work back up." — asserting name-reclamation in the spine narration every player sees. Yet the SAME cell's structured REVEALS field places "Tahei claims his true name (a late, de-emphasised side beat)" on the OPTIONAL Origin track; O5 (line 2097) confirms "Ignoring the Origin track still gives the G6 truth-reveal — you only miss the reunions + buff" and re-states the name there; and §1.4 (148-149) + D-006 (771) LOCK identity as "a late, de-emphasised side beat in the origin thread (identity is explicitly a side thread, not the spine's climax)." The round-1 fix correctly split [THREAD: Tama] (spine) vs [THREAD: Origin] (optional) in the structured field but left the name clause in the spine prose, so a skip-Origin player is shown name-reclamation for a thread they never completed — a literal contradiction with locked canon. Needs-human: the climax is narrative copy (story judgment), and the real choice — guarantee the titular name to all (would require amending locked §1.4/D-006) vs. keep it Origin-gated (drop the clause from line 2027) — is a human design call.
confirmedFindings.old_string: And down-valley: she is real, and grown, and not you. 'Tama ran. Tama lived.' You have a name again: Tahei. You set it down quietly, and pick the house's work back up.
confirmedFindings.new_string: And down-valley: she is real, and grown, and not you. 'Tama ran. Tama lived.' You pick the house's work back up. (The name — Tahei — waits for you among your own people, if you go to them.)
confirmedFindings.question: At the G6 climax, should a player who SKIPS the optional Origin track still reclaim the name "Tahei"? The G6 spine event-log line (prd.md:2027) currently hands the name to every player ("You have a name again: Tahei"), but the same cell's structured field, the O5 capstone (line 2097), and LOCKED canon §1.4/D-006 all place name-reclamation on the optional Origin track only. Options: (A) Canon-default — strip the name clause from the G6 spine line so the spine delivers only the Otsuru truth, leaving "Tahei sets his true name down" on Origin O5; honours locked §1.4/D-006, cheapest. (B) Guarantee the titular identity payoff to all players — move name-reclamation to the spine and amend §1.4, D-006, O5's parenthetical, and §3.6.2 line 2088 to match (reopens locked canon). Trade-off: titular identity payoff for every player vs. honouring the locked "identity is a side thread, not the spine's climax" design.

confirmedFindings.title: Round-1's impossible-gate fix only audited the ESTATE side of the R6/E2 gate — the ARMS side (Arms ≥ 0.4K at R6) is unreachable on the §4.8.1 schedule, and intent-fidelity-1 is understated as a result
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md §4.7.5 line 2904 / §4.8.1 R5-R6 lines 2959-2960 vs §4.2.1 Arms deed cap line 2381 + Arms itemization lines 2412-2413 / seasonal Δs line 2539
confirmedFindings.evidence: Round-1 numbers-balance-1 fixed E2's ESTATE floor (1K→0.6K) but never checked E2's ARMS floor on the same gate: line 2904 E2 = 'Estate ≥ 0.6K + Arms ≥ 0.4K, rank ≥ R6'. Tracing the §4.8.1 Arms accrual: Arms deeds 'BEGIN accruing' only at R5 (line 2959: 'first DEFEND deed (20 ip) + ~6 Arms deeds (Arms→~0.25K)'); R6 adds '~5 Arms deeds' (line 2960). Each T0 Arms deed is capped at 20 ip (line 2381), so R6's 5 deeds add ≤100 ip; the seasonal stream by ~season 6 sums to ~128 ip total (line 2539 Δs: 30+33.1+21+16.7+14.3+12.7). Starting from R5's stated ~0.25K, R6 reaches only ~0.25K + ~0.10K(deeds) + ~0.013K(one season) ≈ 0.36K — SHORT of the 0.4K required to build E2 at R6, which §1.5.1 line 224 and §4.8.1 R6 both schedule. Separately, §4.2.1 (lines 2412-2413) claims the Arms 70% share comes from '30 deeds', but with onset at R5 only R5(~6)+R6(~5)+R7(~5-7) ≈ 16-18 Arms deeds physically fit before the gate — ~40% short of 30.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: Two round-1 findings underweight this: numbers-balance-1's verification stopped at the Estate column and missed that the same gate's Arms requirement is under-supplied; intent-fidelity-1 (rated low) is really the same Arms-accrual shortfall that now also blocks a scheduled estate build (E2 at R6) on the critical path. Taken literally the proposed numbers wall the player at R6. Because the numbers are 'proposed', this is a retune not a hard bug — but it shows the Arms stream is structurally undersized against both its R6 floor and its own 30-deed proof.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Either move Arms-deed onset earlier (let R3-R4 combat kills feed the Arms pillar, not just Combat Standing), raise T0 Arms deed bases / loosen the 0.04 cap for Arms, or lower the R6/E2 Arms floor to ~0.3K. Then re-derive the §4.2.1 Arms deed count so the itemization matches the rung-by-rung schedule.
confirmedFindings.verdict_reason: Confirmed against docs/prd.md §4.8.1: the R5 line (~2959) ends Arms at ~0.25K; the R6 line (~2960) adds only "~5 Arms deeds" yet on the same line requires "Arms ≥ 0.4K" to build E2 (gate also at §4.7.5 ~2904 and §1.5.1). With the T0 Arms per-deed cap = 20 ip (~2381), 5 deeds add at most 0.10K, and one season of the Arms seasonal stream (~2539) adds ~0.01-0.03K, reaching only ~0.36K — short of the required 0.4K. So the E2/Arms floor at R6 is under-supplied by the schedule, an internal inconsistency between adjacent lines that round-1's numbers-balance-1 fix (which only lowered E2's ESTATE floor 1K→0.6K) did not address. The secondary deed-count claim (only 16-18 of 30 Arms deeds fit) is weaker: the 30-deed inventory sums to the R7 0.5K gate and R7's Arms-deed count is unspecified, so R7 can absorb the remainder — but the primary impossible/under-supplied-gate claim holds. Because §4.2 numbers are explicitly "proposed v1 balance — for human review," this is a retune with a real design trade-off (where Arms-deed onset sits, deed bases, or the floor value), hence needs-human, not trivial-apply.
confirmedFindings.question: At R6 the §4.8.1 schedule supplies only ~0.36K Arms (R5 ends at ~0.25K; R6 adds ~5 Arms deeds capped at 20 ip each ≈0.10K, plus ~0.02K seasonal), but the E2 build at R6 requires Arms ≥ 0.4K (§4.7.5/§1.5.1) — so the same gate's Arms floor that round-1 left untouched is unreachable on the stated ramp. How do you want to close the ~0.04-0.05K gap: (a) move Arms-deed onset earlier so R3-R4 combat kills feed the Arms pillar (not just Combat Standing), front-loading the ramp; (b) raise T0 Arms deed bases / the 0.04 per-deed cap so R5-R6 deeds carry more weight; or (c) lower the R6/E2 Arms floor to ~0.3-0.35K? Whichever you pick, the §4.2.1 Arms 30-deed itemization should be re-derived to match the rung-by-rung onset.

confirmedFindings.title: §4.2.2 internally disagrees on WHICH seasons are autumn — the worked tie-out marks seasons 3 & 7 as Au, but the prose says the autumn bump lands on '(4 & 8)' and calls season 8 'the final autumn' (it is winter)
confirmedFindings.location: docs/prd.md §4.2.2 line 2519-2520 vs table lines 2531 & 2535 and ordering line 2523
confirmedFindings.evidence: Line 2523 fixes the order 'Seasons read Sp→Su→Au→Wi ×2', and the worked table marks season 3 ('3 **Au**', line 2531) and season 7 ('7 **Au**', line 2535) as the autumns; season 8 is '8 Wi' (line 2536), i.e. winter. But the prose immediately above contradicts the table twice: line 2519 says the basis reaches '100 % (season 8, the final autumn)' — season 8 is winter, not autumn — and line 2520 says 'Estate's autumn seasons (4 & 8) carry a ~12 % harvest bump' — seasons 4 and 8 are both winter per the table. So the ~12% autumn harvest bump is described as applying to the wrong (winter) seasons in the very section meant to be the rigorous, headlessly-testable tie-out.
confirmedFindings.why_it_matters: This is the canonical worked example other §4 sections are tuned against, and §6.10 says it is regression-tested. If an implementer applies the autumn bump to seasons 4 & 8 (per the prose) instead of 3 & 7 (per the table), the basis ramp and the 30%-telescoping no longer match the table — a silent off-by-one in the season scheduler.
confirmedFindings.suggested_resolution: Correct the prose to match the table/order: autumn = seasons 3 & 7; season 8 is the final (winter) appraisal where the basis happens to peak at 100%.
confirmedFindings.old_string: to **100 %** (season 8, the final autumn); Estate's autumn seasons (4 & 8) carry a ~12 % harvest bump.
confirmedFindings.new_string: to **100 %** (season 8, the tier's final winter appraisal); Estate's autumn seasons (**3 & 7**, marked Au in the table) carry a ~12 % harvest bump.

clusters.theme: Economy/market §4 number-spine is incomplete (coin is a dead-end currency; market engine has zero numbers; resource caps undefined)
clusters.summary: Three lenses converge: §2.4(c) and §3.8 twice promise the market numerics (koku↔coin exchange rate, per-good buy/sell prices or spread, saturation/recovery rates) '→ §4', but §4.7 defines none — coin appears only as flavour, has sources (trade surplus, loot) but no priced sink, and the T1 market that the whole trade economy hangs off cannot be implemented or balanced. Separately §6.5 claims resources carry 'caps' but ResourceDef/GameState store uncapped counts with no cap value or overflow rule. The exploit-loop framing is structurally bounded by the trade ≤⅓ hard invariant and the saturation damper, so this is a completeness gap, not a live exploit — but coin/market/caps are all unspecified balance/data.

clusters.theme: Accrual & gating arithmetic does not tie out (T0 held-koku ledger; R6/E2 Arms floor unreachable; §4.2.2 autumn-season prose vs table)
clusters.summary: §4.0/§4.7.1 assert ~21K koku produced → 3–5K held, implying ~16–18K of sinks, but the only koku-denominated sinks in §4 total 2.4K (E1+E2); food/tools/crafting are koku-free and debt clearance is an Influence GAIN — and the per-rung throughput is labelled 'net' then re-deducted as gross (double-count). Round-1's impossible-gate fix only audited E2's Estate side; the same gate's Arms ≥0.4K floor is unreachable on the §4.8.1 schedule (~0.36K reached at R6). And §4.2.2's worked tie-out prose mislabels seasons 4 & 8 as the autumn harvest-bump seasons when the table marks 3 & 7 (season 8 is winter) — an off-by-one in the canonical regression-tested example (trivially fixable). The master bookkeeping is internally inconsistent.

clusters.theme: v1 narrative integrity at the climax/ending is contradictory or starved
clusters.summary: The single most load-bearing beats are broken. (1) The v1 T3 cliff-hanger stub is doubly-defined: the §5 T3 header alone says it is the Porter-Guild first-contact (already spent at T2-G0/G1), while every other section (G7, §3.5, §3.7.1 C0, §5 T3.2) opens onto the castle-town/Daikan — a builder following the header would ship a confused rerun. (2) The round-1 'climax→spine' fix is incomplete: the G6 spine event-log line hands the titular name 'Tahei' to every player, but the same cell's structured field, O5, and LOCKED §1.4/D-006 gate name-reclamation to the optional Origin track — self-contradictory at the emotional climax of a game titled Kami-kakushi. (3) The locked Naoyuki rivalry→respect→brotherhood arc vanishes for all of T1 (~8h); 'respect' is never beated and v1 jumps straight to a G5 ally-flip — likely reading as unearned.

clusters.theme: Authenticity: real-name leaks & period/terminology slips break the hard-locked 'fictionalise real names' canon
clusters.summary: Two famous real-person/house names appear at the endgame: 'Junkenshi Toyama Saemon-no-jō' is the documented formal name of Tōyama Kagemoto ('Tōyama no Kin-san'), cast in his real wise-judge role and a period slip; and 'Rusui Konoe' uses a premier go-sekke regent court-noble house name on a minor samurai functionary (status anachronism), saturating the entire T4 tier as the sole recurring capital face. Both violate the human-locked A6/§1.13 'no real place/daimyō/house names' rule (round-1 caught Nihonbashi; these are fresh leaks). Plus a low terminology slip: 'Osaka fudasashi' conflates Edo-specific stipend brokers with Osaka's kuramoto/kakeya (§1.7.1 L466 / §3.7 L2175, trivially correctable).

clusters.theme: Combat system underspecified for build (weapon lines, stance roster, retreat, difficulty calibration, recovery location)
clusters.summary: Combat is a first-class pillar but the least-specified system. The LOCKED '2-3 weapon lines' are never enumerated or differentiated (combat math treats every weapon skill identically — a pure reskin). Stances exist only as a TYPE with no concrete roster, and the type vs formulas disagree: defense reads stance.defMod (not declared — trivially addable), attackPower ignores the declared stance.attackMod, and targetCount has no multi-target resolution. Retreat is a first-class verb but its resolution (clock/HP/loot/Arms-dent) is undefined. Difficulty is calibrated/CI-tested at exactly one point (R3 wolf); ~8 later rungs and ~12 mobs have no win-rate target. And the headline 25%→85% recovery is mislocated: §4.6.6 says it happens 'over real R3 play' but the 85% endpoint needs weaponSkill 5 + a crafted blade, both gated to R4–R5.

clusters.theme: Pacing-engine rules missing: soft-stamina floor and simultaneous-reveal serialization
clusters.summary: Two engine-level pacing contracts have no realizing rule. The 'soft stamina never to zero' guarantee has no floor constant or satiety→rate curve anywhere in §4 (it is even absent from the §4.9 levers index), so a naive implementation can asymptote the labour/combat-speed multiplier toward 0 — the crawl-to-halt the system explicitly forbids. And the signature 'act → ONE thing fades in' reveal cadence is undefined at simultaneity: §6.3 reduce lets plural surfaces flip true on one tick with no queue/ordering, so ≥2 predicates can stack multiple panels/log lines at once.

clusters.theme: Accessibility & UX gaps (entry point, mobile IA, ARIA live region, kanji labels, textScale, save-loss, settings drift)
clusters.summary: A11y is asserted as 'cannot be an afterthought' but lacks the wiring/QA to guarantee it. Settings has no always-available entry point at the bare cold open (colourblindMode/mute/pause unreachable). Mobile bottom-bar (3-5 slots) vs ~8-9 revealed nav surfaces has no primary/overflow rule. The 5-channel event log as one ARIA live region has unspecified politeness/scope (would flood AT) and the a11y acceptance runs keyboard+touch only, never a screen reader. Kanji-only labels + aria-hidden emoji + K/M/B abbreviations leave AT users with bare CJK and ambiguous numbers. textScale is offered but no QA tests large-scale reflow and the §3.3 display clamp uses vw units that bypass root-font scaling. Single autosave + import/'fresh start' overwrite with no required confirm or pre-overwrite backup on a no-reset ~28.5h saga. And §2.21(c) settings shapes drift from §6.4 (colorblindMode vs colourblindMode, reduceMotion vs reducedMotion — a silent key-mismatch build bug — plus a persisted 'paused' and over-scope volume sliders).

clusters.theme: QA harness & fun-proxy drift from the PRD spec
clusters.summary: The measurement layer the project self-tests with is inconsistent with the spec it measures. The canonical __qa/QaApi contract (§6.10/§2.20) omits pacing() and reveals() — the exact telemetry methods the fun-proxy regression suite and reveal-cadence proxy are built on (additive, trivially fixable). fun-factor's 'recognised deed every ~4.5-5 min' cadence is a T0-only figure stated as the global proxy/reward-desert guardrail, but the locked T2 budget deliberately delivers ~13 min/deed — a literal reading false-fails the doc's own named slump zone. And the ≥30-min floor proxy says 'a rung' not 'a grind rung', false-failing the blessed ~5-min R0 cold open (trivially qualifiable, and qa-playtesting already scopes it correctly).

clusters.theme: UI-design palette/rate rules contradict PRD canon and the bible's own §2 tokens
clusters.summary: The UI bible has two internal/canon palette conflicts on the signature four-pillar panel. §5.3 illustrates 'always show the rate next to the total' on the House-Influence roll-up (家威 +12/koku), implying the very time-trickle the PRD HARD INVARIANT forbids for Influence. And §6.3's pillar number-jump flashes 'toward --shu on gain / --ai on loss', which spends the reserved vermilion seal colour on ~70% routine deeds and uses --ai (the §2 gain colour) for losses — flatly inverting/colliding with the §2 delta tokens (gain=--ai, loss=--beni) and §6's 'routine ticks stay quiet' rule. Both fixes live in ui-design.md; the PRD body is correct.

clusters.theme: Content scope reconciliation: T2 anti-slump package and quest-type budget
clusters.summary: Two scope mismatches between intent and authored content. The fun-doc's four-part T2 anti-slump 'package' is only half-authored: the front-loaded G0 verb and the fractal re-run-at-scale ARE present, but the seasonal-twist strategy-rotation and the combinatorial cross-pillar combos are unwritten — and the combos directly conflict with §4.3's 'no cross-pillar leakage' invariant. Separately the quest-type budget is self-contradictory: locked at '~4 v1 quest types' with bounty/duel/investigate 'parked for T2+', yet v1 = T0–T2 and T2 authors all three (Hanzaki DUEL, poacher BOUNTY, INVESTIGATE one-shots) — so v1 ships ~7 archetypes while the cut-set advertises 4.

clusters.theme: Verifier & generate-don't-duplicate coverage gaps (the sole release gate misses balance + real-name invariants)
clusters.summary: verify is the only release gate (no hosted CI), yet §6.6 checks only ids/flags/lint — no gate-threshold monotonicity/reachability and no accrual-arithmetic tie-out, so the impossible-gate and deed-count bug classes (found only by human reading across both rounds) slip through automatically. The hard-locked 'fictionalise real names' canon is the one invariant NOT machine-checked — the structural reason real names keep leaking (round-1 Nihonbashi, round-2 Toyama/Konoe). And generate-don't-duplicate is not applied to §4 itself: gen-docs writes only docs/balance/+docs/content/, never the PRD's own hand-typed derived tables (JUDGE_K, deed-sums, XP points), where round-1 found JUDGE_K/XP drift.

  selectors: { unlocked(): SurfaceId[]; tier(): TierId; production(): ... },  // read-only derived reads

  advanceSeason(): void, toRung(id: RungId): void, toTier(n: TierId): void,  // time-compression helpers — fast-forward to a checkpoint (qa-playtesting.md §1)
  selectors: { unlocked(): SurfaceId[]; tier(): TierId; production(): ... },  // read-only derived reads
trivial_fixes.rationale: Adds pacing()/reveals()/time-compression helpers to the canonical __qa contract so the fun-proxy regression suite and reveal-cadence proxy (already specified in qa-playtesting.md §1/§3) have the API they depend on. Purely additive, return shapes already documented in the satellite. Verified old_string at §6.10 line 4242.

The §4.6.1 defense formula (line 2762) reads stance.defMod, but the Stance type (line 1122) does not declare it — an undefined field reference the M2a engine cannot compile. Adding defMod is pure clerical alignment with the formula already committed to the field; no balance/design choice. Verified old_string is the unique Stance shape at line 1122.

Both bots are needed. | 2.2 |
trivial_fixes.rationale: Qualifies the floor proxy to 'grind rung (R1–R7)' so it stops false-failing the blessed ~5-min R0 cold open, matching qa-playtesting.md §3 and the §4.8.1 R0 carve-out. Operates on the full table row (verified at fun-factor.md line 158) to avoid corrupting the markdown cell — the finding's fragment→full-row pairing would have broken the row. Fix is in the satellite doc; PRD body is already correct.
